title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Stakeholder Model Mission
summary: A volunteer event coordinator. The coordinator needs a quantity, percent, staffing, and contingency plan that survives changed attendance. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Community Event Budget with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Estimate magnitudes with benchmarks and defend an appropriate tolerance before calculating exactly.
Test proportionality with tables, graphs, equations, and constant ratios.
Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A price of $110 is reduced by 16%. What is the new price?
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use 110 × (1 − 16/100), then compare the result with the original price.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
